tokens: And the updated (Rover) headings, since you're at the north end of Station 8, will be something like about 240.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I think your rake sample here at the Sculptured Hills is going to have to tell the tale combined with the observation that most of the blocks we saw were, like (the one) Gene sampled, looked like subfloor gabbro.
It's conceivable that the Sculptured Hills could be the same kind of material.
I think it's fairly clear that the boulder population does not resemble the Massif (boulder) population at all.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You been riding on this downslope all the time!?!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you hadn't said anything, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...when you're not holding on with two hands, we'd like the frame count from you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Is that Van Serg over there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Should have a bearing (at Station 9) of 234...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) (To Jack) We got to get around SWP (means "Bowen") here and then ...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: SWP...(correcting himself) or Bowen, I mean.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bowen is out here ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bowen isn't much of a crater on the map.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Heading ought to be about 240; 240 for a heading for there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: But what did he say for bearing and range?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we think you're even farther north than I was saying.
Maybe it's about 215 would be your heading for there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And all the big blocks still look like subfloor from the Rover.
But "big blocks" in here are only about a third of a meter in diameter.
We're out on the plains again now, just off the break in slope (coming down off the Sculptured Hills) (Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That stuff sure looks like outcrop down in the East Massif on the lower slopes, where the high albedo is.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) It was one of my guidelines for the geophone deployment...(correcting himself) (I mean) "guide points".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's some more of that blue-gray rock there on the east end of the South Massif, down low.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It looks like it might have been a slump block or something.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can see it's blue-gray because of it's contrast with the light mantle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It might be a slump block, or something like that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I'm going to go to the left.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's probably Bowen there, don't you think?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, see, we never got too far...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about a range and bearing, guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Maybe we got too far east.
And we're moving along at 10 to 12 clicks.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We haven't had one of those for a couple of (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Got a crater ahead of you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Down-Sun isn't much easier than up-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's just easier on the eyes.
(Chuckles) You just can't see any more, that's all.
(Pause) You don't have that static, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Hope you've still got an (OPS) antenna; I haven't looked recently.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there's no holes in the high-gain.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Might have hit it on a rock (when you fell at Station 8).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Reacting to a rock or crater in their path) Oh, boy!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're back into the mantle area (and the) population of fragments is still one percent or so.
The crater out off to our left, which is at 227 and three point ...What is that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It is a fairly good-sized depression, but it's completely mantled.
There's no blocks showing in the wall at all.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, there's a crater in the wall of that depression or the small one near it.
And it has one big block in the side, as if it penetrated the mantle and exposed some of the wall of the depression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Valley of Taurus-Littrow is not planar!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I'm glad we changed it to a "subfloor" instead of the "plains" unit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're in the inner wall of the depression here, and the rocks still look like subfloor gabbro.
Boy, there's certainly not much variety.
(Pause) In general, there are few exotics ...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Ooh, now that's got to be Cochise.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We think you're coming up on Cochise.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Get yourself a couple pictures while you're looking right at it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, we are on the northeastern rim of Cochise.
I'm going to work my way around the other side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...looking at the western wall of Cochise, I can see a contact within the subfloor between albedo units, one of which is a light tan-gray and the other is a light blue-gray.
(It) may reflect the two kinds of subfloor gabbro we've already sampled.
And that contact looked like it was dipping...(The) apparent dip in the wall was to the north.
In the west wall dipping to the north about 20 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And you got a bearing and range there at the rim of Cochise?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I took a picture of it and...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're at 228/3.0, and we're headed south and not quite on the east rim.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll give you a hack at the east rim.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I got a picture of that contact, so...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) I took some pictures right into Cochise, too, when we were coming up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It'll show on yours, too, probably.
We're sort of on the inner...(Garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, we're sort of inside the east rim a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, don't get too far inside.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're halfway between the rim and where the blocky wall starts.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Cochise is much like Horatio - actually, more like Camelot, although not as blocky in the walls, in general - in that it has blocky walls but a mantled rim.

(Pause) Again, all the blocks I see in here - big ones and blocks down to about 20 centimeters - are subangular, in general, and appear to have the appearance of the subfloor gabbro; although most of the smaller rocks do not appear to be highly vesicular.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I was just about to take a picture, and the minute you take your eye off anything...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I got another view of that contact, and let's put that on the northwest wall of Cochise and dipping to the southeast.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: South and east is to our left.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Put it on the northwest wall dipping to the northeast.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See that, Geno, can you see that over there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can see it now between the gray and blue-gray?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you swing in there, and let me get another shot of it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause; Jack laughs) Now, there's a good view right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay now, I need to have you go left.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got two of them in there, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look at that rock right in front of us.
It looks like a contact between a blue and a gray.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We can't get down to it, but take a picture.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I think we've done...I think we've got that (contact) relationship.
I think we got it at Station 1, as a matter of fact.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But that's a big beautiful boulder on the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's how you bend your tires.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, that's what it (the wire wheel)'s for.
(Pause) Boy, that's a nice...Oh, man, would that be (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, now, that might be glass covered.
That might be a glass coating; the way it sort of hangs on the outside there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wonder if we've lost our fender.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think you're right, because that's just about the right place.
Let's see, 234 and 2.1 is where I want to go, and I'm at 230/2.5.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Our block population in here now - on the south rim of Cochise and out ahead of us - looks like it's up to 5 percent.
(Pause) And it all looks like light, tan subfloor gabbro...or tan-gray.
You don't see much blue-gray; not out on here.
(Pause) There's a recent hit, (a fresh crater).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This Rover is getting tested for what it was built for now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I tell you it handles just the way as advertised, maybe even better.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We think you guys are getting to the point we ought to swing a little bit west to make that 234/2.1.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We have a Tally-ho on Shorty...I mean of Van Serg.
(Bob chuckles) How about through there, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's not pre-judge the crater too much.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And remember we talked about parking on the southeast rim.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It wanders like...Our wander-factor in here has got to be 50 percent.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't tell what you're saying.
(To Gene) Isn't that where we want to go, over there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you see Van Serg, that's what we want.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me wander over that way.
That's where I want to get, but I couldn't go there because of that...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's a different looking rock there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And remember, we're talking about parking on the southeast rim.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Yeah, I think you're going to have to bear right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's why I...I've got to get through this (boulder) field, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're still primarily in an extreme block field here now.
It's up to a 20 percent cover, and...(20 percent cover) of fragments, mostly the subfloor.
Some of it looks quite highly shattered.
I just saw one piece that looked like a white anorthositic rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can go farther up there, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, okay, if you can get up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get a little farther on the southeast.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There is some grayish rocks that are...Oops!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I turn to the right and park right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Returning to his description of the grayish rocks)...that have somewhat of a swirl texture.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I meant...Yeah, bearing is 230/2.2, and I'm parked on a heading of 320, which gives you a better view.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This (seatbelt) is getting harder and harder (to release).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, what's wrong with it, now.
I might have got it twisted.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, let me look at it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Got it hooked, but not so I can get it undone.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, let me look at it.
I'd say "stay put", but I don't think you have any choice.
That fender just curled under, that's where we're getting the dust.
Look at those other fenders, talk about warping.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did I get it (the seatbelt) twisted or something?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, you did twist it when you put it on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) You had one twist in it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, that makes a difference (by shortening the belt).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) It's lost it's stiffness in there.
Okay, I guess nominal plan for Shorty, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt
